display.purpose: Metric card values (Total Customers, MRR, Revenue at Risk).

body.purpose: Default text across tables, dates, descriptions, controls.

shadows.name: lifted-button
shadows.purpose: The Upload button's slight lift off the page.

shadows.name: modal
shadows.purpose: The UploadModal dialog surface, paired with a dark backdrop scrim.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Solid cyan CTA button (e.g. Upload, Send email).
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 8px; background: #0369a1; color: #fafafa; font-size: 14px; font-weight: 500; padding: 8px 16px; border-radius: 8px; border: none; transition: background 0.15s; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: #075985; } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #38bdf8; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Secondary Button
components.css: .ds-btn-secondary { background: transparent; color: #d4d4d4; font-size: 14px; font-weight: 500; padding: 8px 16px; border-radius: 8px; border: 1px solid #404040; transition: background 0.15s; } .ds-btn-secondary:hover { background: #262626; } .ds-btn-secondary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #38bdf8; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Risk Badge
components.description: Pill-shaped risk category indicator (Healthy / Under-utilized / At-risk).
components.css: .ds-badge { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 6px; border-radius: 9999px; padding: 4px 10px; font-size: 12px; font-weight: 500; } .ds-badge-dot { width: 6px; height: 6px; border-radius: 9999px; background: currentColor; } .ds-badge-at-risk { background: #450a0a; color: #f87171; box-shadow: inset 0 0 0 1px #7f1d1d; }

components.name: Sidebar Logo Tile
components.description: Flat bordered cyan icon chip in the sidebar header — deliberately not a gradient.
components.css: .ds-logo-tile { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; width: 36px; height: 36px; border-radius: 12px; border: 1px solid #075985; background: #082f49; color: #38bdf8; }

narrative.overview: Churn-CALC reads like an instrument panel built for someone making real revenue decisions, not a marketing site for an AI feature. The surface is dark and settled, so that the handful of things that actually need attention (a red risk badge, a revenue-at-risk figure, a recommended action) are the only things that visually raise their hand. Cyan is used deliberately and sparingly: bright for text, icons, and active state; a deeper, muted step for solid button fills. This system explicitly rejects the AI-dashboard reflex: no gradient-filled logo tiles, no pastel AI-feature panels, no single accent color doing every job, no decorative side-stripe borders standing in for real hierarchy.
keyCharacteristics: Near-black surfaces (neutral-950 page, neutral-900 cards) with a subtle neutral-800 border.
A deliberate two-step cyan: bright sky-400 for text/icons, deeper sky-700 for solid fills.
Risk severity color is a signal, not a decoration.
Flat by default; depth comes from a border and lighter surface tone, not shadow.

rules.name: The Two-Step Cyan Rule
rules.body: Cyan text/icons and cyan button fills are never the same shade. Bright sky-400 is for anything read directly off a dark surface; the deeper sky-700/sky-800 is reserved for solid fills carrying white text.

rules.name: The One Hue, One Meaning Rule
rules.body: Emerald, amber, and red belong to Healthy, Under-utilized, and At-risk respectively, and to nothing else.

rules.name: The Border-Not-Shadow Rule
rules.body: Depth on a resting surface comes from a border and a lighter fill, never a shadow. Shadow only appears on something genuinely elevated above the page.

dos: Do keep the two-step cyan intact: sky-400 for text/icons, sky-700/sky-800 only for solid fills.
Do use the near-black tint + bright text badge convention for every status or risk indicator, paired with a text label or icon.
Do keep cards flat: border + surface, no drop shadow.
Do give every risk category exactly one meaning across the whole app.
donts: Don't put a gradient on the sidebar logo tile or any icon tile.
Don't give the AI Explanation or Recommended Action panels a softer pastel AI treatment.
Don't lean on cyan as the only accent for everything on a screen.
Don't add a colored side-stripe border to cards, list rows, or callouts.
Don't treat Arial/system-default as a deliberate typographic choice going forward.
